steps.title: Use slope-intercept form
steps.content:
A line with slope 
$$
-\frac{1}{3}
$$ can be written as
$$
y=-\frac{x}{3}+b
$$

steps.title: Find the intercept
steps.content:
Substitute the point $(9,10)$ into the equation.
$$
10=-\frac{9}{3}+b
$$
$$
10=-3+b
$$
$$
b=13
$$

steps.title: Write the equation
steps.content: The line is $$ y=-\frac{x}{3}+13 $$, which has the required slope and passes through $(9,10)$. The answer is D.
